items: New lane metrics measured directly vs your lane opponent from the match timeline: ΔCS, ΔLevel and ΔGold at ~7 and ~14 minutes — a truer 'was I ahead' signal than the old binary Riot laning flag

Every stats view (matchups, blocks, coaching progress, trends) now has a metric column picker; the new Δ metrics are off by default — tick them to show. New games capture the timeline automatically; run ./crawl.sh --backfill-lane-deltas to fill in past games
Fixed duplicate items in the item-build picker (some items appeared twice) — the list now shows one entry per item

One Pager (📄 on a Matchup guide row) — a full-screen, distraction-free quick reference for one matchup: runes, skill order, item build, and your notes. Made for a second screen during a game; Esc closes it
"Champ guide" is now "Matchup guide" throughout the app, with your champion's icon shown next to the selector

Back up everything you've written as one .zip (Settings → Export everything): sessions, block learnings, matchup guides incl. saved skill orders, champion notes, item builds, research entries, plus all uploaded clips and screenshots — and restore it on a fresh install via Import from a backup

Item build per champion on the guide page — core build order (up to 6 items) plus labeled situational sections, picked from real current-patch item icons; included in JSON export/import and the PDF export
Research tab — a study journal for other players' games: freeform Markdown entries (player, champion, opponent) with attached screenshots
Fixed Matchups rows getting stuck on "Loading…" when expanded (a function-name clash with the Champ guide silently broke the games fetch)
Cooldown popup's "💾 Save build" moved to the top-right corner of the dialog, next to the view toggle, where it's easy to spot

Champ guide reorganization: the guide editor now contains the skill order with nested edit/remove controls (read view shows it clean, no stray buttons); the cooldown popup's save is a compact 💾 Save in the skill-order header, fixing the misaligned Q/W/E/R rows between the two sides

Cooldown comparison gets a "Level matrix" view — level × ability grid where each rank-up bubble shows the ability's (haste-reduced) cooldown from that level on; cells stay clickable for editing the build, and the view choice is remembered

Skill order in the cooldown popup is now a full 18×4 grid (level × Q/W/E/R) — click a cell to spend that level's point, so 1-1-1 and 2-1-0 openers and anything else legal works; selections are validated against the in-game rules
Save your build per matchup: the cooldown popup's "Save build to champ guide" stores your skill order on that matchup's champ guide, preloads it next time, and shows it read-only on the guide page
Cooldown comparison (⏱) is also available on Champ guide matchup rows

Champion pool management moved to Settings (replacing the separate "My champion" setting — your pool's first pick is your default champion now); the Blocks view shows a read-only pool summary with an edit shortcut into Settings

Cooldown comparison popup (⏱ on a Matchups row) — your ability cooldowns side by side with the opponent's, with haste sources (items/runes/buffs granting ability haste or ultimate haste) reducing the shown values next to base
Each side has a champion level slider and a drag-to-reorder skill priority (R at 6/11/16) so cooldowns reflect actual ranks at that level — skill order is remembered per champion

Champion dropdowns (Champ guide, default champion, note migration) group your Champion Pool at the top, in pool priority order; the Champ guide also defaults to your pool's first pick when no default champion is set
Champion pool chips can be drag'n'drop reordered — order is the pool's priority (main first, counters in preference order) and is used wherever the pool appears
Champ guide list is table-like: aligned columns, row hover, and separate indicators for matchup notes (📝) and rune pages (🔮)
Fixed partially-filled rune pages silently failing to save (unfilled slots were rejected by validation with a near-invisible error)
Save failures in the Champ guide now show a clear red error message
Switching champion or collapsing a matchup mid-edit now asks before discarding unsaved changes
Rune pages in the Champ guide expand for viewing (▸ on the page card) — full rune names by tree, no need to open the editor
Matchups with a guide now always show in the Champ guide list, even against opponents that champion hasn't faced yet (e.g. migrated or imported notes)
Older matchup notes (pre-champ-guide) now surface in Settings — migrate them under one of your champions, or delete them (replaces the one-time startup popup)
Default champion setting — pre-selects your champion on the Champ guide page

Champ guide design pass: consistent Markdown editor, patch dropdown defaulting to the current patch (validated), properly sized champion icons, opponent picker only lists champions without a guide, clearer empty-state
Clips section design pass: add-clip form now folds behind a + Add clip button and matches the app's form styling
Fixed the Fiddlesticks champion icon never loading (match-v5 vs DDragon spelling mismatch)
One-time prompt after updating: matchup notes written before the champ-guide update are no longer shown in the app — answer yes to export them as Markdown files to a folder of your choice
Champ-guide import now validates rune pages and file structure (clear errors instead of accepting malformed files), and export rejects invalid champion names
Champ guide can be exported as a printable PDF — general notes, patch, rune pages (with icons), and notes for every matchup
Accent color customization in Settings — pick any color for buttons, active tabs, links, and chart lines
Appearance settings — adjustable opacity (see-through panels, like a translucent terminal) and an optional background picture
Runes actually played now show in a block game's expanded stats panel too, mirroring Overview and Champ guide
Removed the 10-game cap on block size — set any block size in Settings
Recent games (Overview and Champ guide) now show the game's time, not just the date
Fixed the packaged Windows app crashing on launch (AttributeError from uvicorn's logging setup touching a None stderr in the windowed/no-console build)
Overview tab's Recent games now records and shows the opponent's runes too, not just yours — click the ▸ Runes toggle on a game to compare both side by side
Crawler now also captures the lane opponent's runes (not just tracked accounts); run ./crawl.sh --backfill-runes again to fill in opponent runes for matches crawled before this update
Overview tab's Recent games table now shows the runes actually played per game
Attach short clips (upload a local video, or paste a YouTube/Twitch link) to a specific part of a coaching session or a specific game in a block — each clip gets its own label
Champ guide matchups are collapsed by default — click a matchup to expand its notes, runes, and recent games, instead of everything loading open at once
Recent games now only fetch when you expand a matchup, not for every matchup up front
Recent games in the Champ guide now show the full rune page actually played (keystone, minors, secondary tree, shards), not just the keystone
Champ guide's matchup rows show a "Recent games" column on the right — real games vs that opponent, with the runes you actually played in each one
Crawler now records the actual rune page played per game for tracked accounts; run `./crawl.sh --backfill-runes` to fill it in for matches crawled before this update
General notes section on the Champ guide page, for champion-wide tips that aren't tied to one matchup
Export/import a champion's whole guide as a JSON file, with an optional password (real encryption, not just obfuscation) — Export/Import menus on the Champ guide page

Champ guide moved to its own page — pick any champion (full roster, not just ones you've played) and see/edit full rune pages, patch, and notes for every matchup it has faced, or add one for a matchup you haven't played yet
Rune picker is now a full click-through builder — primary tree, keystone, 3 minor rows, secondary tree, 2 secondary minors, and 3 stat shards, with real rune icons
A matchup can hold more than one rune page (e.g. alternatives you're testing)
Champ guide is scoped per (your champion, opponent) instead of just the opponent — Gwen vs Darius and Camille vs Darius get separate guides
Matchups table links out to the Champ guide page for the selected "My champion" filter
Champ guide tab on matchups — record a rune keystone + secondary tree, patch version, and Markdown notes on how to play each matchup
Blocks auto-close when a newly added game is too far (game time) from the block's latest game — threshold in Settings (default 3 h, 0 = off), with an optional confirmation prompt (on by default)
Settings page grouped into sections with a sticky, more visible Save bar
Block notes in matchups render as Markdown (all notes now do, everywhere)
Patch notes panel — this one! 📋 in the top bar
Adjustable block size in Settings (1–10 games, default 3)
Matchups table marks champions that have block notes (🧱) alongside manual notes (📝)
Block notes in the matchup overview can expand the block's learnings inline
Account selector: multi-select dropdown in the top bar, works in every view, tier-colored, 'All accounts' default
Blocks can be closed early (with confirmation; irreversible)
Coaching sessions always shown in full; stats scope to the selected accounts
Block notes vs each champion shown inside the matchup overview, with links to their blocks
MIT license
'Hide my rank / LP' setting hides your own rank everywhere, including graphs
Matchup overview split into two columns: notes left, win/loss timeline right
Win/loss bars click through to that game's details
Rolling 10-game graph of lane won @7/@14 min per matchup
Matchups moved to their own tab with per-matchup notes (Markdown) and win/loss timeline
Rank chart estimates LP between snapshots from ranked wins/losses (fainter line)
Rank over time chart on the Overview with a vertical line for every coaching session
